####  [Evaluation Committee for the RFQ No. 26-011Q Comprehensive Vulnerability Assessment (Grant Funded) ](https://boynton-beach.org/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=245)
The Evaluation Committee for the RFQ No. 26-011Q Comprehensive Vulnerability Assessment (Grant Funded) will meet in City Hall Room 115 on the date and time noted below, to discuss and rank proposals as part of the evaluation and selection process. [Read on...](https://boynton-beach.org/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=245)

####  [UPDATE #3: Utilities lifts precautionary boil water notice ](https://boynton-beach.org/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=225)
As of 3 p.m. today, September 8, 2025, the City of Boynton Beach has lifted the precautionary boil water notice for all customers living east of I-95, including the Towns of Hypoluxo, Ocean Ridge and Briny Breezes. [Read on...](https://boynton-beach.org/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=225)

[Read on...](https://boynton-beach.org/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=224)

####  [UPDATE: City of Boynton Beach Utilities issues precautionary boil water notice east of I-95 ](https://boynton-beach.org/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=223)
The City of Boynton Beach has restored water services as of September 6, 2025, following our contractor and crews working through the night to repair a damaged water main. [Read on...](https://boynton-beach.org/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=223)

City of Boynton Beach utilities is issuing a precautionary boil water notice for customers in utilities service areas east of I-95. [Read on...](https://boynton-beach.org/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=222)
